pattern: To keep the appended total minimal, always reach for the smallest positive integer not already present, skipping over any value already taken.
hints: The cheapest k integers to add are just the smallest k positive integers, minus whichever of those are already used elsewhere in the array.
Sort (or dedupe into a set) the existing values, then walk 1, 2, 3, ... skipping any that already appear, until you have picked k of them.

Sum of the smallest k free integers = sum(1..k) restricted to a run, adjusted for how many of 1..k+extra are already occupied; walking gap-by-gap over sorted unique values and filling the gaps with the arithmetic-series formula avoids an O(k) loop when k is huge.

edge_inputs.description: k larger than the used range, forcing a big contiguous run
nums already exactly 1..n, so every append starts after n
duplicate entries must only block one integer each, not one per occurrence
nums has no small values at all, so 1..k are all free
large k requires the closed-form sum, not an O(k) walk

stress.description: 10000 random existing values with large k, exercising the gap-skipping logic under load
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 10**9 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, 10**8 + 1)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: no special structure needed; the closed-form solution must stay fast even with huge k
